title: Mapping Earth’s Observations, featuring Betsy Ford
summary: NASA’s Earth-observing satellites track an enormous range of phenomena: how aerosols move through the atmosphere, how moisture descends through soil, how land-cover shifts over decades. It’s some of the most consequential data NASA produces, informing science, policy, agriculture, and climate research around the world. title: NextSTEP-3 A: Lunar Enabling Technology

title: NASA Seeks Industry Input to Accelerate Lunar Surface Technologies
summary: Long-term lunar exploration requires technology, infrastructure, and operations that function together cohesively on the surface of the Moon. title: This Month at ESA: June 2026
summary: What did space have in store for Europe this month? This June, ESA astronaut Luca Parmitano was named pilot of NASA's Artemis III mission, Ariane 6 set a new European launch record, Proba-3 returned to operations, ESA satellites detected early signs of El Niño, Euclid unveiled its most detailed view yet of the Milky Way's galactic centre, and Sophie Adenot reached the halfway point of her εpsilon mission aboard the International Space Station.
